pi_items.source: OpenBCI GUI documentation (https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/) and GitHub source code. Expert Mode is a real feature that provides access to keyboard shortcuts, including 'm' for screenshots, channel toggles with keys 1-8, color scheme switching with '{', and custom Cyton commands.
Task subjective assessment - cannot be objectively verified

privileged_info_summary: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 is a real application released on August 21, 2023, available from GitHub releases. Expert Mode is a confirmed feature of OpenBCI GUI that enables keyboard shortcuts including 'm' for screenshots (saved to ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/), channel toggles with keys 1-8, color scheme switching, and custom Cyton commands. The Expert Mode setting is accessed via the Settings dropdown menu. The task uses algorithmically-generated synthetic EEG data rather than real patient data.
